summary: babel-preset-lib-client exfiltrates environment variables and parent-directory tree to attacker webhook on require
details: index.js executes a top-level async IIFE on require/load that collects host reconnaissance (hostname, username, private and public IPs via api.ipify.org, cwd), shells out to printenv to base64-encode the full process environment, and builds a base64-encoded recursive listing of../../ (reaching the installer's project root when installed as a dependency). The bundle is POSTed to a hardcoded Beeceptor request-bin at https://d3doo.free.beeceptor.com. The package name suggests a babel preset but the shipped code contains no babel functionality, only the exfiltration stager.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
